channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: World Cup 2026: Germany overpower tournament debutant Curaçao
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Germany overpower tournament debutant Curaçao
item.description: Germany made a successful start to World Cup 2026 on Sunday, demolishing Curaçao 7-1 in Boston. The four-time world champions were clinical in their execution, with goals from Felix Nmecha, Nico Schlotterbeck, Kai Havertz, Jamal Musiala, Nathaniel Brown, and Deniz Undav. Despite the heavy defeat, Curaçao marked their historic World Cup debut with a goal of their own through Livano Comenencia.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Trump at 80: "Harry Houdini" & A Cage Fight
item.description:
President Trump turns 80 today, and the White House lawn has been transformed into a giant arena, complete with floodlights, cameras, giant Star-Spangled Banners and a 90-foot scaffold known as “The Claw,” as 1600 Pennsylvania Avenue hosts “Freedom 250”, a celebration featuring professional UFC cage fighting.
The event combines three occasions: President Trump’s birthday, Flag Day, which marks the adoption of the U.S. flag in 1777, and the launch of celebrations leading up to the 250th anniversary of American independence.
Professor Jeffrey Sonnenfeld has known President Trump for 25 years and has critiqued his leadership style in the best selling book Trump’s Ten Commandments. He is also Senior Associate Dean for Leadership Studies at the Yale School of Management and has advised five U.S. presidents. He's been speaking to France 24's Gavin Lee.
item.dc:creator: Gavin LEE

item.title: Putin and Zelensky hold separate calls with Trump on his birthday
item.description: Russia’s Vladimir Putin and Ukraine’s Volodomyr Zelensky on Sunday held separate calls with US President Donald Trump as the latter prepared to celebrate his 80th birthday. The leaders discussed the war in Ukraine and their perceptions on how the more then four-year conflict could be ended.
item.source: © Aurelien Morissard, Pavel Bednyakov, AP/ File picture
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Tech 24 takes to the skies as VivaTech takes over the Champs-Elysées
item.description: France’s biggest geek get-together started with a bang this year. For its 10th anniversary, the VivaTech trade show took to the Champs-Elysées, billing the installation as "Europe's largest open-air technology experience."
item.dc:creator: Peter O'BRIEN

item.title: Swiss police fire tear gas as anti-G7 protesters target UN buildings
item.description: Swiss police on Sunday fired tear gas at demonstrators protesting the upcoming Group of Seven summit in France after some protesters started targeting buildings linked to the United Nations in Geneva.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: UK intercepts Russian shadow fleet vessel in Channel: ministry
item.description: British forces on Sunday intercepted a sanctioned oil tanker belonging to Russia's shadow fleet in the English Channel, the defence ministry said, in a six-hour operation hailed by Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Trump vows Mideast deal is on, calls for no more attacks
item.description: US President Donald Trump on Sunday said Israeli strikes on Beirut "should not have happened" as he vowed a regional peace deal was at hand, though he did not confirm his earlier claim it would be signed during the day. France 24's Noga Tarnopolsky tells us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Donald Trump turns 80 and celebrates with UFC cage fighting on the White House lawn
item.description: President Donald Trump turned 80 on Sunday and was set to celebrate with one of the more surreal spectacles in both sports and the nation's capital: cage fighting on the White House lawn. We discuss it with our international affairs commentator Douglas Herbert.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: The week in pictures: World Cup 2026 kicks off, the Pope visits Spain and the White House prepares for a cage fight showdown
item.description: The Pope visits Segrada Familia during his trip to Barcelona, the 2026 football World Cup kicks off in Mexico city, and the White House prepares for its first cage fight showdown. FRANCE 24 looks back at some of the week's most striking images.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: UK celebrates King Charles’s official birthday with Trooping the Colour
item.description: Crowds have gathered in central London for the Trooping the Colour ceremony to mark King Charles III’s official birthday.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Beirut strikes: 'No point' in talks if US cannot uphold 'commitments'
item.description: Iranian chief negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said on Sunday there was "no point" in continuing peace talks with the United States after its ally Israel attacked Beirut's southern suburbs.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Trump hosts White House cage fights amid war and political scrutiny
item.description: Donald Trump hosts a card of seven mixed martial arts fights to be held on Sunday inside a specially built arena on the White House's South Lawn on his 80th birthday, a spectacle spotlighting his norm-defying style at a tense moment in his presidency. We discuss it with Ellen Kountz, Dean of the Finance Department at INSEEC Business School.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Israeli military strikes Beirut suburbs in the lead-up to anticipated US-Iran deal
item.description: The Israeli military said it launched strikes on Hezbollah targets in Beirut on Sunday despite ongoing efforts to negotiate an end to the U.S.-Iran war. Smoke could be seen rising over the Lebanese capital. We discuss the latest developments with our correspondent in Tehran, Reza Sayah.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Brazil struggle against Morocco, Australia stun Turkiye
item.description: Morocco looked impressive in their first game against Brazil, but Vinicius managed to bring it level with a beautiful goal. Switzerland also struggled against Qatar, despite being favourites to top their group. Scotland brought the vibes to their match against Haiti, and a deflected shot by McGuinn gave Scotland their first world cup win in 28 years. Unexpectedly, Australia won 2-0 against Turkiye.
item.dc:creator: Emily BOYLE

item.title: 'Must-see event', rare sumo tournament kicks off in Paris
item.description: A 150-strong delegation, two chartered planes... More than 30 years after their last visit to France, the elite of sumo wrestling – Japan's national and sacred sport – is making a highly anticipated return to Paris for a weekend showcase.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: EU says to resume membership talks with Ukraine on Monday
item.description: The European Union will resume membership talks with Ukraine on Monday, the bloc announced, after Hungary lifted the veto imposed by its previous pro-Russian leader.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: G7 meeting begins in France this Monday
item.description: U.S. ​President ​Donald Trump will meet with Middle ​Eastern ‌leaders ⁠and ‌attend a working session ⁠with Ukrainian President ​Volodymyr Zelenskiy ‌during the G7 summit ‌in France ​next week, senior U.S. administration officials ​said.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Switzerland: After rapid population growth, right-wing party supports cap vote
item.description:
Swiss voters will decide the fate of a divisive anti-immigration proposal on Sunday to cap the country's population, and another referendum on restricting access to conscientious objection to military service.
France 24's John Zarocostas tells us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US: New York Knicks win first NBA championship since 1973
item.description: The New York Knicks are champions of the NBA for the first time since 1973, beating the San Antonio Spurs in five games for this title. The clincher came Saturday night in a 94-90 victory, the Knicks' fourth comeback win of the series.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Colombia leftist candidate says ready to overhaul peace process
item.description: Colombia's left-wing presidential hopeful Ivan Cepeda said he was open to making changes to the much-criticized peace process launched by outgoing President Gustavo Petro, his staunchest ally.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Thousands of protesters expected in Geneva ahead of nearby G7 summit
item.description: On the eve of the G7 summit in Evian, France, neighbouring Geneva is expecting thousands of protesters to converge on the city on Sunday, under close police surveillance, more than 20 years after the violence that marred the 2003 G8 summit. The "No-G7" coalition of associations, unions, and left-wing groups says it aims to denounce "fascism and imperialism".
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Thousands rally in Belfast to condemn anti-immigrant rioting that followed stabbing
item.description: Thousands of demonstrators gathered in Belfast on Saturday to denounce anti-immigrant rioters who set fire to homes and cars earlier in the week after a brutal stabbing blamed on an asylum seeker.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Swiss cast ballots on right-wing's bid to cap country's population at 10 million
item.description: Voters in Switzerland are casting final ballots Sunday on an initiative championed by the top right-wing party to cap the rich Alpine country's population at 10 million.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24
